pack_name: KJ Template Pack - Kevin's Custom Designs

usage.selection: Read this compact index first to shortlist KJ templates from metadata.
usage.previews: For title-slide previews, read only the preview_md files for the shortlisted KJ candidates.
usage.final_generation: After the user chooses a KJ template, read exactly that template design_md before generating the full deck.
usage.never: Do not bulk-read templates/*/design.md and do not read template.html unless the selected design.md is missing a critical implementation detail.

frontend_slides_policy.mobile_behavior: preserve 16:9 stage; letterbox or pillarbox instead of reflowing slide content

templates.name: KJ Starter
templates.tagline: Starter template cloned from Blue Professional — edit design.md and preview.md to make it your own.
templates.best_for: Placeholder copy of Blue Professional. Replace this with the contexts your real KJ template is built for once you customize design.md.
templates.avoid_for: Same as Blue Professional until customized: contexts that should feel hot, playful, or intentionally informal.

templates.name: Voltcast
templates.tagline: Acid-green-on-void brutalist hardware-spec deck — screaming Archivo Black uppercase, JetBrains Mono // readout labels, one electric accent, hard wire grids, zero rounded corners.
templates.best_for: Hardware, dev-tools, synth/audio, crypto/protocol, and any technical product launch that wants to feel engineered and loud rather than corporate. Strong for spec sheets, changelogs, roadmap decks, and founder keynotes with attitude. Also an unexpectedly good underdog pick for a security, infra, or fintech deck that wants brutalist authority over soft polish.
templates.avoid_for: Warm, soft, or human-centered material (healthcare, education, lifestyle, anything requiring approachability) where high-chroma acid-on-black reads as cold or aggressive.

templates.name: Redline
templates.tagline: Swiss/Bauhaus poster meets technical spec sheet — pure white, screaming Archivo-black headlines, a single hot-red carrying every accent, hard 2px bordered boxes, numbered mono eyebrows.
templates.best_for: Reports, internal reviews, strategy/operating decks, dev-tool and infrastructure onboarding, and any technical or B2B story that wants crisp Swiss authority over soft polish. Also a strong underdog pick for a finance or research deck that wants to feel rigorous and modern, or a product launch that wants confident structure without going dark or neon.
templates.avoid_for: Warm, playful, or luxury/lifestyle material where pure-white-plus-hot-red reads as severe or industrial.

templates.name: Duolingo Learn
templates.tagline: Bright gamified presentation deck — lowercase rounded Nunito, Feather Green hero, chunky 3D pressable buttons with colored bottom shadows, circular lesson nodes, a winding-path agenda, green-checkmark bullets, XP pills, and a labeled color-psychology accent system (yellow=XP, orange=streaks, blue=Super, red=hearts). Talk register, not landing-page.
templates.best_for: Education, learning products, habit/streak apps, kids/EdTech, community and onboarding decks, and any product update or recap that wants to feel fun, motivating, and approachable rather than corporate. Strong for gamification-themed talks, growth/retention reviews framed as 'leveling up,' and content-rich internal presentations (product reviews, all-hands, OKR recaps) that want energy and warmth over polish. Carries real talk content — agendas, bulleted points, comparisons, metrics dashboards, process flows, takeaways.
templates.avoid_for: Formal, enterprise, luxury, legal/financial-gravitas, or somber material where bright saturated greens, rounded lowercase type, and emoji read as unserious or childish. Also avoid for marketing/landing-page decks that need conversion CTAs — this system is intentionally a talk register.

templates.name: Stagehand Dev
templates.tagline: Developer-tools landing-page system — dashed-border graph-paper grid, one golden-yellow accent, and isometric hatched cubes.
templates.best_for: SDK launches, dev-tool and API pitches, technical documentation decks, open-source project intros, infra/platform overviews, and engineering all-hands. Also a strong, unexpected pick for a fintech or data-product deck that wants to read as rigorous and built rather than glossy — anywhere a 'made by engineers' register beats marketing polish.
templates.avoid_for: Warm, emotional, or consumer-lifestyle decks and expressive brand storytelling — the flat, technical, single-accent register reads as cold there.

templates.name: Hedgehog OS
templates.tagline: Charming macOS-inspired glassmorphism — frosted 'app windows' with traffic-light titlebars on a warm-beige desktop lit by orange/purple/green glows, a Finder-style explorer sidebar, glass widgets, a floating AI-coach chat, and one friendly-orange accent. Geist + Geist Mono.
templates.best_for: Dev-tool and SaaS product launches, design-system and platform overviews, onboarding and changelog decks, and any technical pitch that wants to feel like a friendly, well-made app rather than a corporate slideshow. Strong for product demos, internal tool intros, and 'we built a thing' engineering decks. Also an unexpectedly good pick for an education or community deck that wants warmth and polish without going playful-cartoon.
templates.avoid_for: Somber, luxury, formal-financial, or cinematic dark material where soft glass, rounded corners, friendly orange, and emoji read as light or unserious.

templates.name: Twenty SaaS
templates.tagline: Refined high-density SaaS CRM deck — monochrome Inter product surfaces, EB Garamond italic accents, soft bordered app chrome, kanban/task/tweet modules, and pastel isometric feature cards.
templates.best_for: SaaS product launches, CRM/data/workflow decks, product strategy, roadmap reviews, GTM operating reviews, investor updates, and internal product narratives that need to feel like a real polished app rather than a generic presentation. Also strong for any "operating system for X" story where dense product surfaces and clean hierarchy are more persuasive than cinematic visuals.
templates.avoid_for: Avoid for emotional consumer storytelling, luxury/editorial fashion, cinematic dark decks, or playful education material where CRM chrome, compact UI density, and restrained monochrome surfaces feel too operational.
